role_title: Programs & Business Operations Lead, Airbnb Services

location.office_time_required: occasional work at an Airbnb office or attendance at offsites, as agreed to with your manager

seniority.level: Senior IC / Lead (likely Staff or Principal-equivalent)
level_signals: 10+ years of experience leading high-growth businesses
Proven track record of building and scaling new businesses
Ability to advise management on complex projects or issues
Comp range $168K-$203K base, consistent with senior IC / lead level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience leading high-growth businesses, with a demonstrated ability to influence and align functional teams.
Proven track record of building and scaling new businesses.
Strong analytical abilities combined with a deep understanding of guest needs and market dynamics. Ability to distill complex information into clear business strategies.
Deep understanding of econometric and statistical analysis, and experience in applying these skills to derive insights from broad data sets.
Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to engage and influence stakeholders at all levels, including executive leadership.
Expertise in driving collaboration across diverse teams, fostering transparency, accountability, and a unified vision.
Comfortable navigating ambiguity and working independently to execute complex programs. A strategic thinker with a passion for innovation and new product development.
Proven experience in leading strategic and tactical execution within a large organization, with the ability to advise management on complex projects or issues.
Capable of self-learning and developing tools to enhance processes, interpreting business risks, and recommending best practices.
Strong enthusiasm for working closely with the Airbnb community, consistently demonstrating Airbnb's core values and fostering a sense of belonging.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Marketplace experience, particularly within a digital company, is preferred.

scope.surface: Airbnb Services business — supply and demand analysis across countries, travel corridors, and customer segments
scope.business_model: marketplace, B2C and B2B (hosts and guests), new services business beyond core stays
primary_metrics_inferred: Key business, financial, and customer metrics for the Services business
Dashboard benchmarks across markets and customer segments
Leading indicators of key business outcomes
core_responsibilities: Conduct advanced analysis to extract actionable insights that inform strategic decisions across key geographical markets
Translate data-driven insights into actionable operational plans that enhance the performance of the Services business
Define new metrics and identify leading indicators of key business outcomes
Build dashboards to track key performance metrics across markets and customer segments to establish benchmarks
Build and maintain strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders
Structure incentives, business models, and ways of working to allow the business to be nimble and enable efficient scale

compensation.equity_form: This role may also be eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits.

green_flags: US-Remote Eligible — broad geographic flexibility within the US
Clear product surface and business unit (Airbnb Services)
Bonus and equity mentioned as part of total package
Employee Travel Credits — relevant perk for a travel-industry role
red_flags: Must live in a state where Airbnb, Inc. has a registered entity — excluded states list is dynamic and not enumerated in the JD
Visa sponsorship status is not addressed in the JD, making eligibility unclear for non-citizen candidates
10+ years requirement is a hard floor that limits the candidate pool significantly
Role title says 'Lead' but JD does not clarify whether this involves people management, which is ambiguous for leveling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + 10+ years requirement)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — no direct reports or people management language found)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and marketplace context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as KPIs)
location.work_auth (inferred as no-sponsorship based on state-registration language; not explicitly stated)
ambiguities: Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; work_auth set to no-sponsorship based on the registered-entity/state-eligibility language, but this is an inference rather than an explicit statement.
The role is titled 'Lead' which can imply people management at some companies, but the JD contains no language about managing direct reports — treated as individual contributor here.
Pay range note says 'job titles may span more than one career level,' suggesting this could be leveled as L5 or L6 depending on internal banding.
